step_by_step_thinking:
The lower two parts of the sternum (sternal body) typically fuse together during early to mid-adolescence, which aligns with option C. At approximately age 12, this fusion process begins and continues through late adolescence. 

I choose Option C: 12 years. This timing corresponds well with when significant bone maturation occurs as part of overall skeletal development during puberty. **
